calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, Memo Of P&A And Declaration Of Pltf
Off Calendar Jan-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kaitlin Preston, Esq. 916-702-3200 of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP for Defendant through Court Call.
No other Appearances.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PAUL O'GRADY's Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment (CCP 473(b)).

Plaintiff's motion for relief from summary judgment is denied. Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. section 473 provides relief from judgment when it is taken because of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Plaintiff does not show that he is entitled to relief under section 473.

First, a mistake of fact or law is "not a ground for relief under section 473, subdivision (b), when the court finds that the mistake is simply the result of professional incompetence, general ignorance of the law, or unjustifiable negligence in discovering the law." (Hearn v. Howard (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1206, internal citations eliminated.) Plaintiff's allegations that his attorney failed to perform basic functions like timely filing is a claim of professional incompetence, not of mistake under section 473.

Second, excusable neglect may provide a basis for relief only when a "reasonably prudent person under the same or similar circumstances" would have made the same error. (Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Grp. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 258, internal citations eliminated.) However incompetent representation is not excusable, and thus neglect does not provide Plaintiff with a basis for relief. "Conduct falling below the professional standard of care, such as failure to timely object or to properly advance an argument, is not . . . excusable. To hold otherwise would be to eliminate the express statutory requirement of excusability and effectively eviscerate the concept of attorney malpractice." (Id., internal citations eliminated; see also Cochran v. Linn (1984) 159 Cal.App.3d 245, 251-252 [affirming denial of motion to vacate summary judgment where plaintiffs' counsel was relying on the fact that they were negligent in preparing their case].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Continued To Jun-20-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS MATTHEW CONWAY, JOSHUA MCADAM, ANTHONY HEALY-LONDON, BURGERS 355 LLC DBA BRASS TACKS'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants' notice of motion seeks at one point to strike 7:16-17 and 8:21 from "Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint," but at another point the notice refers to plaintiff's "Second Amended Complaint." Further, plaintiff's second amended complaint is unclear as to which causes of action purportedly support punitive damages and from which defendant(s) punitive damages are sought. For efficiency's sake, I GRANT defendants' motion but with leave to re-plead. If plaintiff elects to re-plead, his third amended complaint must clearly state at the start of each cause of action which defendant(s) that particular cause is pleaded against. As to punitive damages, plaintiff must plead all of the requirements for them in each cause of action in which they are sought and he is to clearly state what defendant(s) punitive damages are sought against.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Lee, Esq. 415-863-4336 for Plaintiff.
Plaintiff Paul O'Grady is also present.
Zachary Price, Esq. 415-281-7634 for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues, submits to demurrer and requests motion to strike be denied. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JOSHUA MCADAM, ANTHONY HEALY-LONDON's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Matthew Conway, Joshua McAdam, Anthony Healy-London, Burgers 335 LLC dba Brass Tacks's unopposed motion to strike is granted. The demurrer of Joshua McAdam and Anthony Healy-London to the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff Paul O'Grady shall have 15 days to amend his complaint to allege (if possible) that McAdam and Healy-London are directly or individually liable for Mr. Coway's and/or Burgers 355 LLC's tortious conduct. Plaintiff must clarify whether McAdam and Healy-London are being sued solely as alter egos of Burgers 335 LLC or not. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
